title: Measuring and Recording Radial Pulse and Counting Respirations
description: Measure and record the radial pulse rate, rhythm, and strength, and count respirations.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Locate the radial pulse on the thumb side of the person's wrist. Use your first 2 or 3 middle fingertips.
Press fingers firmly enough to feel the pulse easily. Note if the pulse is strong or weak and regular or irregular.
When the pulse can be felt plainly, look at the second hand on your watch and count the beats for 60 seconds.
Keep your fingertips on the resident's wrist. Do not tell the resident you are counting respirations.
Begin counting when the chest rises. Count each rise and fall of the chest as 1 respiration.
Count respirations for 1 minute.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report and record the pulse and respirations and your observations. Report abnormal results at once.

title: Measuring and Recording a Height When Lying in Bed
description: Measure and record the height of a resident who is lying in bed.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Gather measuring tape and ruler.
Wash hands.
Provide for privacy.
Position the resident flat in bed if this position is allowed.
Have a co-worker hold the end of the measuring tape at the resident's heel.
Pull the measuring tape alongside the resident's body until it extends past the head.
Place a ruler flat across the top of the resident's head. It should extend from the resident's head to the measuring tape. Make sure the ruler is level.
Determine the resident's height.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Record the measurement in its proper place.

title: Measuring and Recording Weight
description: Measure and record the weight of a resident using a standing or wheelchair scale.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash hands and provide for privacy.

For Standing Scale: (a) Make sure resident is wearing nonskid shoes that are fastened before walking to the scale. (b) Start with the scale balanced at zero before weighing the resident. (c) Help resident to step onto the center of the scale. Be sure the resident is not holding, touching or leaning against anything. (d) Determine the resident's weight. Balance the scale by making the balance bar level. (e) Help resident to safely step off scale.

For Chair or Wheelchair Scale: (a) Start with the scale balanced at zero. (b) Help the resident transfer into the chair scale, or roll the wheelchair onto the scale. (c) Place the resident's feet on the platform of the scale. (d) Determine the resident's weight. Subtract the weight of the wheelchair from the total. (e) Help the resident off the scale.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Record the measurement in its proper place.

title: Making an Unoccupied Bed
description: Make a clean, wrinkle-free unoccupied bed using proper linen handling and mitered corners.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash hands.
Collect clean linens: bottom sheet, drawsheet (if needed), waterproof under-pad (if needed), top sheet, blanket, bedspread, pillow case for each pillow, bath towel, gloves, and laundry bag.
Place linens on a clean surface. Use the bath towel as a barrier between the surface and clean linens.
Raise the bed for body mechanics.
Put on gloves.
Remove linens. Roll each piece away from you. Place each piece in a laundry bag. (Discard incontinence products and disposable bed protectors in the trash.)
Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Move the mattress to the head of the bed.
Place the bottom sheet on the mattress. Unfold it lengthwise. Place the center crease in the middle of the bed.
Open the sheet. Fan-fold it to the other side of the bed.
Tuck the corners of a fitted sheet over the mattress at the top and then foot of the bed. The sheet is tight and smooth.
Place the drawsheet on the bed in the middle of the mattress. Open the drawsheet and fan-fold it to the other side. Tuck the drawsheet under the mattress.
Go to the other side of the bed.
Pull the bottom sheet tight so there are no wrinkles. Tuck in the sheet.
Pull the drawsheet tight so there are no wrinkles.
If using a waterproof under-pad, place the under-pad on the bed in the middle of the mattress.
Go to the other side of the bed.

Put the top sheet on the bed: unfold lengthwise, place center crease in the middle, place hem even with top of mattress, open and fan-fold to the other side, face hem stitching outward away from the resident.

Place the blanket on the bed: unfold with center crease in the middle, put upper hem about 6 to 8 inches from the top of the mattress, open and fan-fold to the other side, turn the top sheet down over the blanket.

Place the bedspread on the bed: unfold with center crease in the middle, put upper hem even with the top of the mattress, open and fan-fold to the other side, make sure the side facing the door is even and covers all top linens.

Tuck in top linens together at the foot of the bed so they are smooth and tight. Make a mitered corner: the bottom sheet is tucked under the mattress at the foot; the side of the sheet is raised onto the mattress; the remaining portion is tucked under the mattress; the raised portion is brought off the mattress.
Put the pillowcase on pillow. Fold extra material under the pillow at the seam end of the pillowcase.
Place the pillow on the bed. The open end of the pillowcase is away from the door.
Provide for comfort.
Attach the call light to the bed or place it within the resident's reach.
Lower the bed to a safe level.
Complete a safety check of the room.
Wash hands.

title: Making an Occupied Bed
description: Change bed linens while the resident remains in bed, ensuring comfort and safety throughout.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash hands.
Provide for privacy.
Gather supplies: pillow cases, blanket or bedspread, top sheet, draw sheet, protective pad if resident is incontinent, bottom/fitted sheet, bag for soiled linen, bath blanket, and gloves.
Place clean linen on a clean surface near the bed.
Adjust bed to a safe level, usually waist height. Lower head of bed. Lock bed wheels.
Put on gloves.
Loosen top linen from the end of the bed.
Unfold bath blanket over the top sheet to cover the resident. Remove top sheet. Keep the resident covered at all times with the bath blanket.
Help the resident to turn onto her side, moving away from you. You will make the bed one side at a time.
Loosen bottom soiled linen on the working side.
Roll bottom soiled linen toward the resident, soiled side inside. Tuck snugly against the resident's back.
Place and tuck clean bottom linen. Smooth the bottom sheet out toward the resident. Be sure there are no wrinkles. Tuck it under the resident's body.
If using a waterproof bed protector, unfold it and center it on the bed. Tuck the side near you under the mattress. Smooth it out toward the resident. Tuck as you did with the sheet.
Have the resident turn to completely the other side. Explain to the resident that she will roll over a bump and assure the resident that she will not fall.
Move to the opposite side of the bed.

Loosen the soiled linen. Check for any personal items. Roll linen from head to foot of the bed. Avoid contact with your skin or clothes. Place it in a hamper or bag. Never put it on the floor or furniture. Never shake it.
Pull the clean linen through. Start with the fitted sheet. Pull and tuck just like the other side. Pull and tuck in waterproof bed protector if used. Finish with bottom sheet free of wrinkles.
Ask resident to turn onto her back. Help as needed. Keep resident covered and comfortable, with a pillow under her head.
Unfold the top sheet. Place it over the resident and center it. Ask the resident to hold the top sheet. Slip the bath blanket or old sheet from underneath. Put in the hamper or bag.

Place a blanket over the top sheet. Match the top edges. Tuck the bottom edges of top sheet and blanket under the bottom of the mattress. Make hospital corners on each side. Loosen the top linens over the resident's feet. Fold the top sheet over the blanket about six inches at the top.
Remove the pillow. Do not hold it near your face. Remove the soiled pillowcase by turning it inside out. Place it in the hamper or bag.
Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Change the pillow cases.
Raise the head of the bed to a level of comfort.
Lower bed to lowest position.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.

title: Giving a Complete Bed Bath
description: Provide a complete bath to a resident in bed, bathing the entire body from head to toe.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy. Be sure the room is at a comfortable temperature and there are no drafts.
Gather your equipment: bath blanket, washcloths (2-4), towels (2-4), bath basin, soap, lotion, deodorant, nail brush or orangewood stick, 2 pairs of gloves, comb or brush, and resident's clothing.
Adjust bed to a safe level, usually waist high. Lock bed wheels.
Place a bath blanket over the resident. Remove or fold back top bedding.
Remove gown while keeping the resident covered with bath blanket. Place gown in hamper or bag.
Fill the basin with warm water. Have resident check water temperature to see if it's comfortable. Adjust if necessary. Change the water when it becomes too cool, soapy, or dirty.
Put on gloves.
Ask the resident to participate in washing. Help when needed.
Uncover only one part of the body at a time. Place a towel under the part being washed.

Wash, rinse, and dry one part of the body at a time. Start at the head, work down, and complete the front first. Use a clean area of the washcloth for each stroke. Eyes/Face: wash face with wet washcloth (no soap), beginning with the eye farther away, wash inner to outer. Arms: use long strokes from the shoulder down to the wrist, rinse and pat dry. Hands: wash in basin, clean under nails, rinse and pat dry, apply lotion to elbows and hands. Chest: place towel across chest, pull blanket to waist, lift towel only enough to wash chest, rinse and pat dry. Abdomen: wash the abdomen, rinse, and pat dry. Legs: place towel under leg, wash thigh with long downward strokes, rinse and pat dry. Feet: place foot into basin, wash foot and between toes, rinse and pat dry, apply lotion to feet especially heels (not between toes). Back: wash back and neck with long downward strokes, rinse and pat dry, apply lotion.

Place a towel under the buttocks and upper thighs. If the resident is able to provide perineal care, place a basin of clean warm water, washcloth, and towel within reach. If the resident cannot provide perineal care, provide it yourself (covered separately). Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
If providing perineal care, wash hands and put on clean gloves. Provide privacy at all times.
Cover the resident with the blanket.
Empty, rinse, and dry bath basin. Return to storage area.
Place soiled linen in proper containers.
Remove and discard gloves.
Wash your hands.
Provide deodorant. Brush or comb the resident's hair. Help resident put on clean clothing. Help resident into comfortable position with proper body alignment.
Return bed to lowest position. Remove privacy measures.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes in resident to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Giving a Partial Bed Bath
description: Provide a partial bath to a resident in bed, washing the face, hands, underarms, and perineal area.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy. Be sure the room is at a comfortable temperature and there are no drafts.
Gather your equipment: bath blanket, washcloths (2-4), towels (2-4), bath basin, soap, lotion and deodorant, nail brush or orangewood stick, 2 pairs of gloves, comb or brush, and resident's clothing.
Adjust bed to a safe level, usually waist high. Lock bed wheels.
Place a bath blanket over the resident. Remove or fold back top bedding.
Remove gown while keeping the resident covered with bath blanket. Place gown in hamper or bag.
Fill the basin with warm water. Have resident check water temperature to see if it's comfortable. Adjust if necessary. Change the water when it becomes too cool, soapy, or dirty.
Put on gloves.
Ask the resident to participate in washing. Help when needed.
Uncover only one part of the body at a time. Place a towel under the part being washed.

Wash, rinse, and dry one part of the body at a time. Start at the head, work down, and complete the front first. Eyes/Face: wash face with wet washcloth (no soap), beginning with the eye farther away, wash inner to outer. Abdomen: wash the abdomen, rinse, and pat dry. Back: wash the back and neck with long downward strokes, rinse and pat dry, apply lotion.

Place the towel under the buttocks and upper thighs. If the resident is able to provide perineal care, place a basin of clean warm water, washcloth, and towel within reach. If the resident wants you to leave the room, remove and discard gloves, wash hands, and leave supplies and call light within reach.
If the resident cannot provide perineal care, provide it yourself (covered separately). Wash hands and put on clean gloves. Provide privacy at all times.
Cover the resident with the blanket.
Empty, rinse, and dry bath basin. Return to storage area.
Place soiled linen in proper containers.
Remove and discard gloves.
Wash your hands.
Provide deodorant. Brush or comb the resident's hair. Help resident put on clean clothing. Help resident into comfortable position with proper body alignment.
Return bed to lowest position. Remove privacy measures.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes in resident to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Giving a Shower or Tub Bath
description: Assist a resident with bathing in a shower or tub, ensuring safety and comfort throughout.
steps: Wash your hands.

Collect the following equipment and place in shower room: washcloths (2-4), towels (2-4), soap and shampoo, clean clothes and non-skid footwear, 2 pairs of gloves, lotion and deodorant, comb or brush, and resident's clothing.
Provide for privacy.
Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Go to resident's room. Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Provide for privacy. Be sure the room is at a comfortable temperature and there are no drafts.
Help resident remove clothing and footwear. Transport resident to shower or tub room.
Help resident into the shower or tub depending on their level of mobility.
Fill the tub halfway with warm water, or prepare the shower stall. Test water temperature. Adjust if necessary. Check temperature throughout the shower.
Put on gloves.
Let resident wash as much as possible. Help to wash and rinse the entire body from head to toe.
Uncover only one part of the body at a time when needed. Use a clean area of the washcloth for each stroke.
Wash, rinse, and dry one part of the body at a time. Start at the head, work down, and complete the front first.
Help wash and rinse any areas the resident cannot reach.
Help resident wash his or her face last.
Help resident out of tub and onto a chair, or roll resident out of the shower.
Give resident a towel and help pat dry. Pat dry under breasts, between skin folds and between toes.
Apply lotion and deodorant as needed.
Remove and discard gloves.
Help resident dress and comb hair before leaving the shower room. Put on nonskid footwear.
Return resident to room.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes in resident to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Brushing Teeth
description: Assist a resident with brushing teeth to maintain oral hygiene and prevent dental disease.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Collect the following: toothbrush, toothpaste, mouthwash, cup with cold water, cup for mouthwash, emesis basin, towel, and gloves.
Arrange items nearby.
Raise the bed for body mechanics, usually waist high.
Raise the head of the bed to have the resident in an upright sitting position.
Put on gloves.
Place a towel over the resident's chest.
Wet toothbrush. Put on a small amount of toothpaste.
Clean each tooth smoothly, including the tongue and all surfaces of teeth and the gum line. Use short strokes. Brush back and forth. Brush tongue.
Give the resident water to rinse the mouth. Place emesis basin under the resident's chin. Have resident spit water into the emesis basin.
Give the resident mouthwash to rinse the mouth. Place emesis basin under the resident's chin. Have resident spit into the emesis basin. Wipe the resident's mouth with the towel.
Remove the towel and dispose of in its proper place.
Clean and return items to their proper place. Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Lower the bed to a safe and comfortable level for the resident.
Unscreen the resident.
Complete a safety check of the room.
Wash hands.
Report and record your observations.

title: Mouth Care for the Unconscious Resident
description: Provide oral care to an unconscious resident using sponge swabs to prevent aspiration.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Collect the following: sponge swabs, towel, emesis basin, mouthwash with a cup, cup of water, lip moisturizer, and gloves.
Arrange items nearby.
Raise the bed for body mechanics, usually waist high.
Put on gloves.
Turn resident on their side with the resident's head turned well to the side.
Place a towel under the cheek and chin. Place the emesis basin next to the cheek and chin for excess fluid.

Dip swab into mouthwash. Squeeze excess solution to prevent aspiration. Wipe teeth, gums, tongue, and inside surfaces of the mouth. Remove debris with the swab. Change swab often. Repeat this until the mouth is clean.
Rinse with clean swab dipped in water. Squeeze swab first to remove excess water.
Remove the towel and basin. Pat lips or face dry if needed. Apply lip moisturizer.
Remove the towel and dispose of in its proper place.
Clean and return items to their proper place. Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Reposition the resident for comfort.
Tell the resident you are finished.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Lower the bed to a safe and comfortable level for the resident.
Unscreen the resident.
Complete a safety check of the room.
Wash hands.
Report and record your observations.

title: Mouth Care for the Resident with No Teeth
description: Provide oral care to a resident without natural teeth using sponge swabs and mouthwash.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Collect the following equipment: sponge swabs, towel, emesis basin, mouthwash with a cup, cup of water, and gloves.
Arrange items nearby.
Raise the bed for body mechanics, usually waist high.
Raise the head of the bed to have the resident in an upright sitting position.
Put on gloves.
Place a towel over the resident's chest.
Dip swab into mouthwash. Wipe teeth, gums, tongue, and inside surfaces of the mouth. Remove debris with the swab. Change swab often. Repeat this until the mouth is clean.
Rinse with clean swab dipped in water. Squeeze swab first to remove excess water.
Remove the towel and dispose of in its proper place.
Clean and return items to their proper place. Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Reposition the resident for comfort.
Lower the bed to a safe and comfortable level for the resident.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Unscreen the resident.
Complete a safety check of the room.
Wash hands.
Report and record your observations.

title: Shampooing the Resident's Hair
description: Shampoo and condition a resident's hair, either in bed or at the sink, removing tangles and styling.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.

Collect the following: 2 bath towels, washcloth, shampoo, hair conditioner (if requested), pitcher or hand-held nozzle (if needed), shampoo tray (if needed), basin or pan (if needed), waterproof pad (if needed), gloves (if needed), comb and brush, and hair dryer (if needed).
Arrange items nearby.
Raise the bed for body mechanics for a shampoo in bed.
Wash hands.
Cover the resident's chest with a bath towel.
Brush and comb the hair to remove snarls and tangles.
Position the resident for the method used. For a shampoo in bed: lower the head of the bed, remove the pillow, and place the waterproof pad and shampoo tray under the head and shoulders.
Support the head and neck with a folded towel if necessary.
Obtain water. Water temperature should be no more than 105°F. Have the resident check the water temperature.
Put on gloves (if needed).
Ask the resident to hold a washcloth over the eyes. It should not cover the nose and mouth.
Use the water pitcher or nozzle to wet the hair.
Apply a small amount of shampoo.
Work up a lather with both hands. Start at the hairline. Work toward the back of the head.
Massage the scalp with your fingertips. Do not scratch the scalp with your fingernails.
Rinse the hair until the water runs clear.
Apply conditioner. Follow directions on the container.
Squeeze water from the resident's hair.
Cover the hair with a bath towel.
Remove the shampoo tray, basin, and waterproof pad.
Dry the resident's face with a towel. Use the towel on the resident's chest.
Help the resident raise the head if appropriate. For the resident in bed, raise the head of bed.
Rub the hair and scalp with the towel, gently. Use the second towel if the first one is wet.
Comb the hair to remove snarls and tangles.
Dry and style hair.
Remove and discard gloves (if used). Wash hands.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Lower the bed to a safe and comfortable level for the resident.
Unscreen the resident.
Complete a safety check of the room.
Clean, rinse, dry, and return equipment to its proper place. Clean the comb and brush. Wear gloves for this step. Discard disposable items.
Dispose of used linen.
Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Report and record your observations.

title: Brushing and Combing Hair
description: Brush and comb a resident's hair, removing snarls and tangles and styling as the resident prefers.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Ask the resident how to style hair.
Collect the following: comb and brush, bath towel, and other hair care items as requested.
Arrange items on the bedside stand.
Assist the resident to a sitting position.
Place a towel across the resident's back and shoulders or across the pillow.
Ask the resident to remove eyeglasses. Put them in the eyeglass case. Put the case inside the bedside stand.

Brush and comb hair that is not matted or tangled: (a) use the comb to part the hair, (b) part hair down the middle into 2 sides, (c) divide 1 side into 2 smaller sections, (d) brush one of the small sections toward the hair ends, and repeat for the other side.

Brush or comb matted or tangled hair: (a) take a small section of hair near the ends, (b) add small sections of hair as you work up to the scalp, (c) comb or brush through each longer section to the hair ends.
Style the hair as the resident prefers.
Remove the towel.
Let the resident put on the eyeglasses.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Lower the bed to a safe and comfortable level for the resident.
Remove hair from the brush or comb. Clean and return hair care items to their proper place. Wear gloves for this step. Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Unscreen the resident.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Dispose of used linens.
Wash hands.

title: Providing Nail Care
description: Soak, clean, trim, and file a resident's fingernails and apply lotion to the hands.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Gather equipment: basin with warm soapy water, basin with clear water, towel, nail file or emery board, orangewood stick, clippers, lotion, and gloves.
Put on gloves.
Soak the resident's hands in the soapy water. Soaking softens thick, brittle nails.
Clean under the fingernails with orangewood stick.
Rinse hands in the clear water.
Dry thoroughly.
Clip fingernails carefully. Cut nails straight across and round edges with nail file or emery board.
Apply lotion to the hands.
Empty, rinse, and dry basin. Return items to designated space.
Place soiled linen in hamper or bag.
Remove and discard gloves.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Document procedure.
Report any observations to the nurse.

title: Providing Foot Care
description: Soak, wash, and dry a resident's feet and apply lotion to maintain skin integrity and comfort.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Gather equipment: basin, soap, towel, washcloth, lotion, and gloves.
Put on gloves.
Place a barrier on the floor and soak the resident's feet in a basin with warm water.
Wash the resident's feet with soap. Wash the top, bottom, and in between toes.
Rinse the resident's feet.
Dry thoroughly.
Apply lotion to the feet. Do not apply lotion between the toes.
Empty, rinse, and dry basin. Return items to designated space.
Place soiled linen in hamper or bag.
Remove and discard gloves.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Document procedure.
Report any observations to the nurse.

title: Shaving with a Disposable Safety Razor
description: Shave a resident's face using a disposable safety razor, following proper technique to prevent cuts.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Find out the resident's preference about using shaving cream and aftershave lotion.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Gather equipment: gloves, shaving cream, disposable safety razor, basin of water, washcloth, towel, and aftershave lotion.
Assist the resident to a sitting position.
Cover the resident's chest area with a towel.
Apply gloves.
Wash resident's face. Do not dry.
Place the warm washcloth to the face for a few minutes.
Apply shaving cream to resident's beard area. Allow to soften beard for a few minutes.
Keep the skin taut and shave in the direction of hair growth — down on the cheeks and upward strokes on the neck and chin.
Rinse razor in between strokes to keep optimal amount of blade exposed.
When possible, have the resident pull his upper lip down to make the area under the nose taut and easier to shave.
Wash off any remaining shaving cream and dry with a towel.
Apply aftershave lotion if requested.
Remove and discard gloves.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Document procedure.

title: Shaving with an Electric Razor
description: Shave a resident's face using an electric razor with pre-shaving and aftershave lotion.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Find out the resident's preference about using pre-shaving lotion and aftershave lotion.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Gather equipment: shaving lotion, electric razor, towel, and aftershave lotion.
Assist the resident to a sitting position.
Cover the resident's chest area with a towel.
Plug in the razor.
Apply pre-shaving lotion to help remove any oils from the face.
Using a circular motion and pressing the razor firmly against the skin, shave each area of the resident's face until smooth.
Apply aftershave lotion if requested.
Remove and discard gloves.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Document procedure.

title: Assisting with Post-Mortem Care
description: Provide respectful care to a resident's body after death, preparing it for the family and funeral director.
steps: Wash hands.
Collect the following: gown, waterproof bed protector, wash basin, bath towel and washcloths, denture cup, tape, dressings, gloves, and valuables envelope.
Provide for privacy.
Raise bed for body mechanics.
Make sure the bed is flat.
Put on gloves.
Position the body supine. Arms and legs are straight. A pillow is under the head and shoulders.
Close the eyes. Gently pull the eyelids over the eyes.
Insert dentures if facility policy. If not, put them in a labeled denture cup.
Close the mouth.
Remove all jewelry, except for wedding rings. List the jewelry that you removed. Place the jewelry and list in a valuables envelope.
The nurse will remove all catheters, tubes, etc. and discard per policy.
Bathe soiled areas with plain water. Dry thoroughly.
Place a waterproof bed protector under the buttocks.
Remove soiled dressings. Replace them with clean ones.
Put a clean gown on the body. Position the body as in step 7.
Brush and comb the hair as necessary.
Cover the body to the shoulders with a sheet.
Gather the person's belongings. Put them in a bag labeled with the person's name.
Remove supplies, equipment, and linen. Straighten the room. Provide soft lighting.
Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Let the family view the body. Provide for privacy. Return to the room after they leave.
Cover the body with the sheet.
Leave the denture cup with the body.
Pull the privacy curtain around the bed. Or close the door.
Remove and discard gloves. Wash hands.
Report the following: the time the body was taken by the funeral director, what was done with jewelry and other valuables and personal items, and what was done with dentures.

title: Transferring From Bed to Wheelchair with One Assist
description: Safely transfer a resident from the bed to a wheelchair using a gait belt with one assistant.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Wash hands.
Gather equipment: wheelchair, transfer belt, non-skid footwear.

Place wheelchair at the head of the bed, facing foot of bed, or at the foot of bed, facing head of bed. The arm of the wheelchair should be almost touching the bed. Place it on the resident's stronger or unaffected side.
Remove footrests.
Lock wheelchair wheels.
Raise the head of the bed. Adjust bed level to lowest position. Lock bed wheels.
Assist resident to sitting position with feet flat on the floor. Let resident sit a few minutes to adjust to the change in position.
Put nonskid footwear on resident and fasten securely.
Stand in front of resident. Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees.

Place the transfer belt around resident's waist over clothing (not on bare skin). Tighten the buckle until snug — you should be able to insert flat fingers comfortably under the belt. Check that skin or skin folds are not caught under the belt. Grasp belt securely on both sides with hands in upward position.

Provide instructions to allow resident to help with the transfer, such as: 'When you start to stand, push with your hands against the bed.' 'Once standing, reach for the chair with your stronger hand.'
With your legs, brace resident's lower legs to prevent slipping by placing one or both knees in front of the resident's knees.
Count to three to alert resident. On three, with hands grasping the transfer belt on both sides and moving upward, slowly help resident to stand.

Tell the resident to take small steps in the direction of the chair while turning his back toward it. If more help is needed, help the resident pivot to stand in front of the wheelchair with the back of his leg against it.
Ask resident to put hands on wheelchair armrests if able. When the chair is touching the back of the resident's legs, help him lower himself into the chair.
Reposition resident so that his hips touch the back of the wheelchair seat.
Attach footrests. Place the resident's feet on the footrests. Gently remove the transfer belt.
Remove privacy measures.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Moving a Resident with a Lift Sheet
description: Use a lift sheet with a team member to safely move a resident up in bed using proper body mechanics.
steps: Ask a team member to assist you.
Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Adjust bed to a safe level, usually waist height. Lock bed wheels.
Lower the head of bed to make it flat. Move pillow to the head of the bed.
Stand on one side of the bed. Your team member stands on the other side.
Stand with a broad base of support. Point the foot near the head of the bed toward the head of the bed. Face that direction.
Roll the sides of the lift sheet up close to the resident.
Grasp the rolled-up lift sheet firmly near the resident's shoulders and buttocks. Make sure you support the resident's head.
Bend your hips and knees.
Slide the resident up in bed on the count of '3.' Shift your weight from your rear leg to your front leg.
Unroll the lift sheet. Straighten the linens.
Put the pillow under the resident's head.
Return bed to its lowest position. Remove privacy measures.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.

title: Turning a Resident to a Side Lying Position
description: Safely turn a resident onto their side while maintaining proper alignment and comfort using positioning devices.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Adjust bed to a safe level, usually waist height. Lock bed wheels.
Lower the head of bed.
Stand on side of bed opposite where resident will be turned.
Move resident to side of bed nearest you.
Cross resident's arm over her chest. Move arm on side resident is being turned to out of the way. Cross leg nearest you over the far leg.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, with one foot slightly in front of the other. Bend your knees.
Place one hand on the resident's shoulder. Place the other hand on the resident's nearest hip.
Gently roll resident onto side as one unit toward the other side of the bed. Shift your weight from your back leg to your front leg.

Position the resident properly: (a) head supported by pillow; (b) shoulder adjusted so resident is not lying on arm or hand; (c) top arm supported by pillow; (d) back supported by supportive device; (e) top knee flexed; (f) supporting device between legs with top knee flexed — knee and ankle supported.
Return bed to its lowest position. Remove privacy measures.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Document the procedure.

title: Transferring From Bed to Wheelchair with Two Assists
description: Safely transfer a resident from the bed to a wheelchair using a gait belt with two assistants.
steps: Ask a team member to assist you.
Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Wash hands.
Gather equipment: wheelchair, transfer belt, non-skid footwear.

Place wheelchair at the head of the bed, facing foot of bed, or at the foot of bed, facing head of bed. The arm of the wheelchair should be almost touching the bed. Place it on the resident's stronger or unaffected side.
Remove footrests.
Lock wheelchair wheels.
Raise the head of the bed. Adjust bed level to lowest position. Lock bed wheels.
Assist resident to sitting position with feet flat on the floor. Let resident sit a few minutes to adjust to change in position.
Put nonskid footwear on resident and fasten securely.
Place the transfer belt around resident's waist over clothing (not on bare skin).
Provide instructions to allow resident to help with transfer.
With your legs, brace resident's lower legs to prevent slipping.
Grasp the transfer belt on each side.
The CNA closest to the chair stands in a position to step or pivot around smoothly to allow the resident access to the chair.
Count to three to alert resident. On three, with hands still grasping the transfer belt on both sides and moving upward, slowly help resident to stand.
Tell the resident to take small steps in the direction of the chair while turning his back toward it.
Ask resident to put hands on wheelchair armrests if able. When the chair is touching the back of the resident's legs, help him lower himself into the chair.
Reposition resident so that his hips touch the back of the wheelchair seat.
Attach footrests. Place the resident's feet on the footrests. Gently remove the transfer belt.
Remove privacy measures.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Transferring a Resident Using a Mechanical Lift
description: Use a mechanical lift with a team member to safely transfer a non-weight-bearing resident from bed to chair or wheelchair.
steps: Ask a team member to assist you.
Knock before entering the resident's room.
Address the resident by name.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure before starting and during the procedure.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Gather equipment: wheelchair and mechanical lift with sling.
Lock bed wheels.
Position wheelchair next to bed. Lock brakes.
Roll resident to one side of bed. Position the sling under the resident, with the edge next to the resident's back. Fanfold if necessary.
Make the bottom of the sling even with the resident's knees. Help the resident roll back to the middle of the bed. Pull out the fanfold edge of the sling.
Roll the mechanical lift to bedside. Make sure the base is open to its widest point. Push the base of the lift under the bed.
Place the overhead bar directly over the resident.

With the resident lying on his back, attach straps to the overhead bar. Have a coworker support the resident at the head, shoulders, and knees while being lifted. The resident's arms should be folded across the chest. Make sure all straps are connected properly and are smooth and straight.
Following manufacturer's instructions, raise the resident two inches above the bed.
Have coworker help support and guide the resident's body. Roll the lift so that the resident is positioned over the chair or wheelchair.
Slowly lower the resident into the chair or wheelchair. Push down gently on the resident's knees to help the resident into a sitting, rather than reclining, position.
Undo the straps from the overhead bar to the sling. Remove sling or leave in place per facility policy and care plan.
Be sure the resident is seated comfortably and correctly in the chair or wheelchair.
Remove privacy measures.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Assisting with Ambulation
description: Safely assist a resident with walking using a gait belt, supporting their weaker side and monitoring for fatigue.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Adjust bed to lowest position so that the feet are flat on the floor. Lock bed wheels.
Before ambulating, put nonskid footwear on the resident and securely fasten.
Stand in front of and face the resident. Place your feet about shoulder-width apart.
Place gait belt around the resident's waist over clothing (never on bare skin). Grasp belt securely on both sides.
If resident is unable to stand without help, brace (support) the resident's lower extremities.

Hold the resident close to your center of gravity. Provide instructions to allow resident to help with standing. Tell the resident to lean forward, push down on the bed with his hands, and stand on the count of three. On three, with hands grasping the gait belt on both sides and moving upward, slowly help the resident to stand.

Walk slightly behind and to one side of resident for the full ordered distance while holding onto the gait belt. If the resident has a weaker side, stand on the weaker side. Ask resident to look forward, not down at the floor, during ambulation.
After ambulation, help resident to the bed or chair. Remove gait belt. Check that the resident is in proper alignment.
Leave bed in lowest position.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes to the nurse.
Document the procedure.

title: Assisting with Ambulation using a Cane or Walker
description: Assist a resident to ambulate safely using a cane or walker, staying on the weaker side and monitoring for obstacles.
steps: Knock before entering the resident's room.
Identify the resident.
Introduce yourself by name and title.
Explain the procedure to the resident.
Wash your hands.
Provide for privacy.
Adjust bed to lowest position so that the feet are flat on the floor. Lock bed wheels.
Before ambulating, put nonskid footwear on the resident and securely fasten.
Stand in front of and face the resident. Place your feet about shoulder-width apart.
Place gait belt around the resident's waist over clothing (never on bare skin). Grasp belt securely on both sides.
If resident is unable to stand without help, brace (support) the resident's lower extremities.

Help as needed with ambulation: (a) Cane — resident places cane about six inches in front of his stronger leg; brings weaker leg even with cane; then brings stronger leg forward slightly ahead of cane. Repeat. (b) Walker — resident picks up or rolls the walker and places it about six inches in front; all four feet or wheels must be on the ground before stepping forward; the walker should not be moved again until the resident has moved both feet forward and is steady; the resident should never put his feet ahead of the walker.
Walk slightly behind and to one side of resident. Stay on the weaker side if resident has one. Hold the gait belt.
Watch for obstacles in the resident's path. Ask the resident to look forward, not down at the floor, during ambulation.
Encourage resident to rest if he is tired.
After ambulation, help resident to the bed or chair. Remove gait belt. Check that the resident is in proper alignment.
Leave bed in lowest position.
Provide for comfort.
Place the call light and other needed items within reach.
Complete a safety check on the room.
Wash your hands.
Report any changes to the nurse.
Document the procedure.
